chapter_context.summary: Azariah the prophet encourages Asa; Asa enacts sweeping national reforms, deposing Queen Mother Maachah for making a detestable idol.

outline.text: Azariah the Prophet Urges Asa: 'The LORD is with you, while ye be with him'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Azariah the Prophet Urges Asa: 'The LORD is with you, while ye be with him' (15:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Asa's Sweeping Reforms; National Covenant Sworn at Jerusalem
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Asa's Sweeping Reforms; National Covenant Sworn at Jerusalem (15:8-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Asa Deposes Queen Mother Maachah for Making an Idol
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Asa Deposes Queen Mother Maachah for Making an Idol (15:16-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: The Temple Centered Kingdom
key_themes.description: The presence of God's glory in the Temple is the absolute center of national blessing and life.

key_themes.title: Immediate Retribution and Reform
key_themes.description: Good kings who seek the LORD experience immediate victory and peace, while unfaithfulness leads to immediate warning and exile.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 15 prompt you to humble yourself, seek God's face, and turn away from compromise?
What practical reforms can you make in your daily walk to ensure your heart is dedicated to God?

cultural_historical.title: The Levitical Worship System
cultural_historical.text: Unlike the Book of Kings, Chronicles highlights the roles of singers, Levites, and liturgical musicians in the temple's daily administration.
